description: Implementation plan for Favor of the Pharaoh digital game. Pure C# game engine with MVC separation â€” Unity is View only. Legacy code is throwaway. Phases 1-3 are parallelizable by agent swarm. References rules in FotP-Rules-v20, game loop in FotP-GameLoop, containers in FotP-SmartLists.

nodes.title: Phase 1: Core Object Model
nodes.description: Pure C# classes with no Unity dependency. Define the singleton game objects and the SmartList container that holds them. All objects are plain C# â€” interfaces for anything needing a future View.

children.title: SmartList<T> Container
children.description: Generic container supporting: add/remove objects, multi-membership (same object on multiple lists), cascade delete (destroying object removes from all lists), parent-child ownership (destroying parent cascades to children). This is the foundational data structure. Ref: FotP-SmartLists plan for all container instances.

children.title: Die Object
children.description: Properties: value (1-6), dieType (Standard/Immediate/Serf/Noble/ Artisan/Intrigue/Voyage/Decree), customFaces (array of face values or special symbols), isLocked, isTemporary, tempPipModifier. Methods: Roll() returns random face, MaxValue(), HasPipValue() (false for Intrigue ** and Voyage symbols when locked). Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 dice-types-and-faces.

children.title: Tile Object
children.description: Properties: name, level (3-7), color (Yellow/Blue/Red), isArtifact (red tiles), isArtifactUsed. Children: list of Ability objects (SmartList parent-child). Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 tile catalogs.

children.title: Ability Object
children.description: Properties: triggerType (StartOfTurn/AfterRoll/LockedAny/AllLocked/ Acquire/Select/EndOfTurn/OnSpecificRoll), dieTypeFilter (Standard/ Immediate/Custom/Active/Locked/Any), isPerTurn, isPerRoll, isArtifact, isUsedThisTurn, isUsedThisRoll. Abstract method: Execute(gameState, player). Child of a Tile (cascade delete). Ref: FotP-SmartLists ability-containers.

children.title: Scarab Object
children.description: Properties: type (Reroll/Pip/Die). Methods: Apply(die, gameState). Reroll: re-roll one active die. Pip: add +1 to die value (max 6). Die: create temporary standard die and roll it. Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 scarab-token-system.

children.title: Player Object
children.description: Owns SmartLists: ownedTiles, dicePool, scarabs. Derived views: ownedByColor (yellow/blue/red), ownedByLevel, bonusDiceSources. Properties: name, pyramidScore (sum of locked dice pip values). Ref: FotP-SmartLists per-player-state.

children.title: Criteria Objects
children.description: Abstract base with Evaluate(lockedDice) -> bool. Subclasses: NOfAKind(n), Straight(length), SumGreaterEqual(threshold), AllDifferent, AllEven, AllOdd, AllDiceInRange(min,max), Pair, TwoPairs, ThreePairs, FullHouse, PairOfValue(v), CompoundCriteria(list of criteria). Each bar slot has one. Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 level-bar-criteria-a-side.

nodes.title: Phase 2: Game State Machine
nodes.description: The game loop controller. Pure C# with event-driven state transitions. No Unity. Drives the turn sequence, fires triggers, manages the market.

children.title: GameState Controller
children.description: Singleton managing: turnOrder (SmartList of Players), currentPlayer, gamePhase (Setup/Playing/RollOff/GameOver), market (bar slots with tile stacks), tokenSupply, roundNumber. Methods: Setup(), StartRound(), NextPlayer(), EnterRollOff(), DetermineWinner(). Ref: FotP-GameLoop plan for full flow.

children.title: TurnStateMachine
children.description: Per-turn controller. States: StartOfTurn -> Rolling -> Locking -> ScarabUse -> ContinueDecision -> Claiming -> PostClaim -> EndOfTurn. Manages dice zone transitions (Cup->Active->Locked), roll counting, lock validation (must lock >= 1). Ref: FotP-GameLoop turn phases.

children.title: Trigger/Event System
children.description: When state transitions occur, fire triggers to matching abilities. FireTriggers(triggerType, player) -> collect all abilities with matching triggerType from player's owned tiles -> execute each. Queue system for abilities that need player input (choose a die). Ref: FotP-SmartLists triggered-abilities-queue.

children.title: Market Manager
children.description: Manages tile stacks per bar slot. Methods: GetClaimableTiles(player, lockedDice) evaluates criteria for each non-empty stack, filters by color-per-level restriction. ClaimTile(player, tile) pops from stack, pushes to player's ownedTiles. Ref: FotP-SmartLists tile-containers.

children.title: Dice Zone Manager
children.description: Manages die movement between SmartLists: Cup, Active, Locked, Set. Methods: RollAllInCup(), LockDie(die), UnlockDie(die) [rare], AutoLockImmediateDice(), CollectAllToCup(). Validates lock rules. Ref: FotP-SmartLists die-containers.

nodes.title: Phase 3: Tile Abilities
nodes.description: Implement all ~55 tile abilities as Ability subclasses. Each is a pure C# class with Execute() logic. Highly parallelizable â€” each tile can be implemented independently once the Ability base class exists. Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 tile catalogs (levels 3-7).

children.title: Level 3 Tile Abilities (11 tiles)
children.description: Farmer, Guard, Indentured Worker, Serf, Worker, Beggar, Servant, Soothsayer, Ankh (artifact), Ancestral Guidance (artifact), Omen (artifact + extra turn). Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 level-3-tiles.

children.title: Level 4 Tile Abilities (11 tiles)
children.description: Artisan, Builder, Noble Adoption, Palace Servants, Soldier, Grain Merchant, Entertainer, Match Maker, Good Omen (artifact + extra turn), Palace Key (artifact), Spirit of the Dead (artifact). Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 level-4-tiles.

children.title: Level 5 Tile Abilities (11 tiles)
children.description: Charioteer, Conspirator, Overseer, Ship Captain, Tomb Builder, Head Servant, Master Artisan, Priest, Bad Omen (artifact, affects others), Burial Mask (artifact), Royal Decree (artifact, roll-off). Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 level-5-tiles.

children.title: Level 6 Tile Abilities (11 tiles)
children.description: Embalmer, Estate Overseer (stored die), Grain Trader, Priest of the Dead, Royal Attendants, Astrologer, Priestess, Surveyor (dice replacement), Pharaoh's Gift (artifact, redo roll-off), Secret Passage (artifact, extra claims), Treasure (artifact, split claims). Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 level-6-tiles.

children.title: Level 7 Tile Abilities (10 tiles)
children.description: Queen (triggers roll-off), General, Grand Vizier, Granary Master (stored die), Heir, Royal Astrologer, Royal Mother (dice replacement), Queen's Favor (artifact, extra turn + claim), Royal Death (artifact, sets roll-off bar), Royal Power (artifact, extra claims). Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 level-7-tiles.

children.title: Custom Dice Face Abilities (4)
children.description: Artisan * (adjust 1 die), Intrigue ** (adjust 2 dice, no pip when locked), Voyage faces (adjust/reroll/+2dice/lock), Decree * (adjust or borrow tile). Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 custom-dice-special-face-abilities.

nodes.title: Phase 4: Roll-Off
nodes.description: The endgame. Triggered by Queen claim. Extends the state machine with roll-off specific states and logic. Ref: FotP-Rules-v20 final-roll-off, FotP-GameLoop final-roll-off.

children.title: Queen Claim Trigger
children.description: When Queen is claimed: set pharaohHolder, record pharaohScore, transition gamePhase to RollOff after current round completes. Award +1 die tokens to players who missed a turn this round.

children.title: Roll-Off Turn Logic
children.description: Each player gets 1 final turn. Same turn state machine but last turn. Compute max possible score to skip eliminated players. If player's locked dice total >= pharaohScore, transfer pharaoh.

children.title: Queen's Last Chance
children.description: If pharaoh was taken from Queen claimer, they get one more turn. Only fires if pharaohHolder != queenClaimer after all roll-off turns.

children.title: Victory Determination
children.description: pharaohHolder wins. Tie-break: closest to queenClaimer's right in turn order. Announce winner, display final state.

nodes.title: Phase 5: Test Harness / Debug View
nodes.description: Console-based game runner and automated tests. This is the View for development â€” no Unity needed. Validates the entire engine works.

children.title: Console Game Runner
children.description: Play a full game via console I/O. Print game state, prompt for player choices (which die to lock, which tile to claim, which scarab to spend). Supports multi-player hot-seat.

children.title: Unit Tests
children.description: xUnit tests for: criteria evaluation (each subclass), die rolling distribution, SmartList operations (add/remove/cascade), ability triggers, state machine transitions, lock validation.

children.title: Scenario / Integration Tests
children.description: Set up specific board states and verify outcomes. Examples: player with Herder locks a pair -> verify +1 die. Player claims Queen -> verify roll-off triggers. Artifact used -> verify one-time flag. Full game simulation with random choices -> verify no crashes.

children.title: AI Player (Random/Simple)
children.description: Automated player that makes random legal choices. Used for stress testing full games. Can run 1000s of games to find edge cases.

nodes.title: Phase 6: Tile Randomization [lead-engineer]
nodes.description: For "later games" per the rules: randomly deal 1 tile of each matching color per bar slot instead of the fixed first-game setup. Needs a lead-engineer agent to decompose tile pool management, dealing logic, and validation that all slots get valid tiles.

nodes.title: Phase 7: B-side Level Bars [lead-engineer]
nodes.description: Each of the 5 level bars has an A side and B side with different criteria. Need to catalog all B-side criteria from the rules and create corresponding Criteria objects. Needs a lead-engineer agent to decompose from the rulebook.

nodes.title: Phase 8: Unity View Layer [future]
nodes.description: Hook the pure C# engine to Unity rendering. Dice physics, 3D pyramid, tile animations, UI panels. The engine exposes events/callbacks that the View subscribes to. May target modern Unity 6 or Godot.
